column1: CLF-C02,"Domain 3: Cloud Technology & Services","Cloud Technology and Services","## CLF-C02 Domain 3: Cloud Technology and Services
This domain is the ""AWS services catalog"" - you must recognize dozens of services, understand their primary purpose, and match them to use cases.

column4: Full control over OS; traditional applications

column3: PaaS - deploy apps without managing infrastructure

column1: - **General Purpose (M, T)** - Balanced; web servers, small databases
- **Compute Optimized (C)** - High-performance processors; batch processing, gaming
- **Memory Optimized (R, X)** - Large in-memory datasets; real-time analytics
- **Storage Optimized (I, D, H)** - High sequential read/write; data warehousing
- **Accelerated Computing (P, G, Inf)** - GPUs; ML training, graphics

column4: Files, backups, static websites, data lakes
Move petabytes when network is too slow

column1: - **Snowball Edge** (80 TB) - Large data migration; edge computing

column4: Instances can download but aren't reachable
Allow rules only; return traffic auto-allowed
Allow + Deny; must allow return traffic
Routing policies: simple, weighted, latency, failover, geolocation
Caches content at edge locations globally
Quick setup; encrypted; uses existing internet
Push to many subscribers (email, SMS, Lambda)
Routes events between AWS services and SaaS

column3: Infrastructure as Code (IaC) - define resources in templates
API activity logging - who did what, when
Operational management; patch management; session manager
""How is it performing?"" (CPU at 90%; errors increasing)
""Who did what?"" (Alice deleted the bucket at 3:15 PM)
""What changed?"" (SG had port 22 opened at 3:14 PM)

column1: - **Security** - Open security groups; MFA not enabled
- **Fault Tolerance** - Single points of failure; no backups
- ""Deploy web app without managing infrastructure"" -> Elastic Beanstalk
- ""Archive data rarely accessed"" -> S3 Glacier Deep Archive
- This is **34% of the exam** - the broadest domain. Know ~60+ services by name and purpose.
- **S3** is the most versatile service - appears in storage, backup, data lake, static website, and analytics scenarios.
- **Lambda vs. EC2** is the most common compute comparison. Lambda = event-driven, serverless. EC2 = long-running, full control.
- **RDS vs. DynamoDB** is the most common database comparison. RDS = SQL, complex queries. DynamoDB = NoSQL, unlimited scale.
- **SQS vs. SNS** - SQS = queue (one consumer pulls). SNS = notification (push to many).
- **CloudWatch vs. CloudTrail** - CloudWatch = performance. CloudTrail = security audit.
- **Serverless services:** Lambda, Fargate, DynamoDB, S3, Aurora Serverless, Athena, API Gateway, SQS, SNS, Step Functions.
- **""Managed""** means AWS handles patching, backups, scaling. You focus on using it.
- **Snow Family** = physical transfer. ""Petabytes"" + ""network too slow"" -> Snow Family.
- **Direct Connect vs. VPN:** Direct Connect = dedicated, consistent, weeks. VPN = encrypted, internet, minutes.
- **CloudFront** = CDN (caching). **Global Accelerator** = performance (AWS backbone, static IPs).
- **Elastic Beanstalk** is PaaS - upload code, AWS handles everything else."
